alertitem.name: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)
alertitem.desc: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) — Reflected attacks are those where the injected script is reflected off the web server.
alertitem.solution: Phase: Architecture and Design. Use a vetted library or framework that does not allow this weakness.

alertitem.name: Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens
alertitem.desc: No Anti-CSRF tokens were found in an HTML submission form. A cross-site request forgery attack could be used.
alertitem.solution: Phase: Architecture and Design. Use a vetted library that provides a CSRF token per form.

alertitem.alert: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
alertitem.name: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
alertitem.desc: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS).
alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

alertitem.name: Loosely Scoped Cookie
alertitem.desc: Cookies can be scoped by domain or path. Cookies that are scoped to a parent domain may be accessible by the parent domain and all subdomains.
alertitem.solution: Always scope cookies to the most restrictive domain and path possible.
